firstSection.title: From Flat Code to Immersive Worlds: The Next Dimension is Interactive Spatial Design
firstSection.text:
Prior to 2016, I worked as a systems engineer, handling a variety of responsibilities including backend development, while enjoying the challenge of solving technical problems like puzzles.

As I gained experience as a backend engineer, I became increasingly interested in design-related aspects of web development.
I wanted to contribute not only to backend development but also to user experience and visual design, which led me to transition to a web production studio.

As the sole frontend engineer on many web production projects, I took responsibility for a wide range of tasks, including WordPress customization, technical support, and workflow optimization.

I built websites from scratch based on design mockups created in Adobe Illustrator.
During maintenance phases, I also implemented updates based on handwritten notes and verbal requests, often working without dedicated design assets.

Going forward, I would like to further deepen my focus on creative field, which has been the area of expression I have been most interested in so far.

data.title: TOEIC 440
data.description:
Taken at a time when my hearing difficulties and developmental disorders were undiagnosed.
Reading comprehension for explicit information, understanding specifications, and fact-based English text processing were stable without accommodations.

data.title: Practical English Proficiency Test (Eiken), Grade 2
data.description:
At that time, I learned at the Kumon Higher Education Division for speed reading.
Due to the drawbacks of language comprehension, I struggled with the listening and interview sections, leading to a decision not to retake the exam.

data.title: Data Science Mathematics Strategist - Intermediate Level

VisualThinking.text:
As part of exploring my personal roots, I have published a research article on the "Community of Mixed-Heritage Orphans in 1970s Nagoya and Its Cultural Evolution", a history my grandparents were closely involved in.

Given the sensitive nature of this topic, I approached the research with strict objectivity, prioritizing diverse viewpoints.
I gathered a wide range of sources, from rare books to international research papers, carefully cross-checking them to ensure historical accuracy.

Through this project, I learned to view individual events not as detached points, but as elements connected to a larger surface, mapping them within a broader social and structural context.
This structural thinking directly translate into my web development work today.
In my daily practice, I rely on official Google documentation and technical community insights to ensure reliable system operation, maintenance, and optimization.

At the same time, I love using creative medium as a language to express my inner state and connect deeply.
On my days off, drawing inspiration from zines and museum visits, I have worked on various projects, including image processing, digital art, video editing, and creative coding.
I am particularly drawn to spirituality and the inner world, continuously researching and reinterpreting surreal.
Going forward, I want to keep exploring immersive design that stems from this inner world, creating experiences that evoke a rich spectrum of emotions.
